phases.name: Fix Policy Service Test Failures
phases.description: Fix 7 failing tests in services/policy-service/. Issues: 1) TestListPoliciesHandler_Empty - policies field missing or wrong type in response JSON. 2) TestCreatePolicyHandler - returns 500 instead of 201, UUID validation failing with empty string. 3) TestEvaluateHandler_DenyPolicy - null organization_id violates not-null constraint. 4) TestEngineEvaluateRules - 'In' and 'Between' rule operators return false instead of true. 5) TestEngineAppliesToScope - document type matching fails. 6) TestRepositoryCreate - UUID parsing fails with 'admin' string. Fix database schema to allow null organization_id, fix UUID generation in tests, repair rule evaluation logic for In/Between operators, and ensure test fixtures provide valid UUIDs.

phases.name: Fix Compliance Service Tests
phases.description: Fix failing tests in services/compliance-service/main_test.go. Tests show similar issues to policy-service with UUID handling and database constraints. Verify compliance-service has proper test fixtures, valid UUID generation in test data, and correct handling of nullable foreign key columns. Ensure migrations run before tests execute via internal/testutil. Add missing test data for audit_logs, compliance_reports, and control_mappings tables if needed.
success_criteria: Migration test setup verified for compliance tables

phases.name: Create Missing Dockerfiles
phases.description: Create Dockerfiles for 7 services that lack them: api-gateway, analytics-service, compliance-service, organization-service, policy-service, m365-integration-service, and gateway. Use existing Dockerfiles (e.g., deployments/docker/Dockerfile.auth-service) as templates. Each Dockerfile should: use golang:1.24-alpine as base, set WORKDIR /app, copy go.mod/go.sum, run go mod download, copy source code, run CGO_ENABLED=0 go build, and use alpine:latest for final image with the binary. Ensure proper ENTRYPOINT configuration.

phases.name: Fix Port Conflicts
phases.description: Resolve port conflicts documented in Known Issues. Currently analytics-service and compliance-service both default to :8006. Organization-service and policy-service both default to :8007. api-gateway and gateway both use port 8000. Update each service's default SERVER_ADDR constant to use unique ports: analytics-service :8006, compliance-service :8008, organization-service :8009, policy-service :8010, m365-integration-service :8011. Ensure docker-compose.yml external ports match these assignments and don't conflict with each other or with PostgreSQL (5432/15432) and Redis (6379/16379).
success_criteria: All 12 services have unique default ports
docker-compose config shows no port conflicts
All services start simultaneously with docker-compose up
Health checks pass on all service endpoints

phases.name: Update Makefile Build Target
phases.description: Add 6 missing services to Makefile build target: api-gateway, analytics-service, compliance-service, organization-service, policy-service, m365-integration-service. Current Makefile only builds core 5 services. Add entries in the SERVICES array and corresponding build rules. Ensure make build compiles all services to bin/ directory. Add individual build targets (make build-api-gateway, make build-analytics-service, etc.) and clean target to remove all binaries. Verify make build produces all binaries successfully.
success_criteria: bin/ contains all service binaries after build

phases.name: Verify Migration Completeness
phases.description: Verify all database migrations are present and functional. Migration 000028 (rate limit tables) was deleted and replaced with 000035. Check for any gaps in migration sequence. Run migrate -path migrations -database "$DATABASE_URL" up to verify all migrations apply cleanly. Test rollback with migrate down 1. Ensure all services' tables exist: users, sessions, enrollment_tokens, printers, agents, print_jobs, documents, watermarks, notifications, audit_logs, usage_metrics, organizations, org_members, print_policies, policy_rules, compliance_reports, control_mappings, rate_limits. Add missing .up.sql/.down.sql pairs if any tables are missing.
success_criteria: No gaps in migration sequence numbers

phases.name: Add Integration Tests for Extended Services
phases.description: Extend integration test suite in tests/integration/ to cover the 6 newer services: analytics-service, compliance-service, organization-service, policy-service, m365-integration-service, and api-gateway. Add test functions covering: health endpoints, CRUD operations for each service, authentication flows, and error handling. Follow the pattern of existing tests in tests/integration/api_test.go. Ensure tests use testcontainers for real database testing and clean up properly. Add test coverage reporting.
success_criteria: Each of 6 new services has at least 5 integration tests
Tests run in CI pipeline successfully

phases.name: Add Unit Tests for Shared Packages
phases.description: Add unit tests for internal packages with missing coverage. Focus on internal/auth/oidc, internal/auth/saml, internal/multi-tenant/audit, internal/multi-tenant/quota, and internal/shared/ratelimit. Create test files following existing patterns (e.g., internal/auth/jwt/jwt_test.go). Test: JWT token generation/validation, OIDC provider configuration, SAML SSO flow, audit log creation, quota enforcement, and rate limiting logic. Use table-driven tests for multiple scenarios. Mock external dependencies (HTTP clients, database) appropriately.
success_criteria: All new tests follow table-driven pattern

phases.name: Security Hardening and Validation
phases.description: Conduct security audit and hardening. Run gosec on all services: gosec ./... . Fix high-severity issues including: SQL injection vulnerabilities, missing input validation, insecure random number generation, and hardcoded credentials. Verify all secrets come from environment variables. Add security headers middleware (CSP, X-Frame-Options, HSTS). Implement request size limits for file uploads. Add CSRF protection for state-changing operations. Run go mod tidy and verify no vulnerable dependencies with go list -json -m all | nancy sleuth. Add security scanning to CI pipeline.
success_criteria: gosec passes with no high-severity issues
Security headers added to API responses
No known vulnerable dependencies in go.mod

phases.name: API Documentation and Developer Portal
phases.description: Complete API documentation in api-gateway's developer portal. Add OpenAPI/Swagger specifications for all 12 services' endpoints. Generate docs from code annotations or create separate openapi.yaml. Document: authentication flows, request/response schemas, error codes, rate limits, and example requests. Add interactive API explorer via Swagger UI or Redoc. Create getting started guide with curl examples for common operations: register user, create print job, list devices, upload document. Add Postman collection for API testing. Ensure docs stay in sync via automated generation from Go handler annotations.
success_criteria: OpenAPI spec covers all service endpoints
API docs auto-generated from code annotations

critical_issues: 13 failing tests (7 in policy-service, likely more in compliance-service)
Port conflicts prevent simultaneous service startup
6 services lack Dockerfiles for containerized deployment
strengths: Build compiles successfully across all services
Comprehensive internal packages with shared utilities
12 services with clear separation of concerns

rationale: Phase ordering follows STRICT RULES: 1) Build works so skip compilation fix, 2) Tests failing → Phase 1-2 fix policy/compliance test failures (critical blockage), 3) Phase 3-5 address deployment gaps (missing Dockerfiles, port conflicts, Makefile), 6) Phase 6 ensures database migrations complete before extending tests, 7) Phase 7-8 expand test coverage to newer services and shared packages, 8) Phase 9 security hardening before final polish, 9) Phase 10 documentation as final production-ready step. This sequence fixes blockers first, then enables deployment, then validates completeness, then hardens for production.
